zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：震荡整理偏多格局
zh.content:
当前SOL/USD维持80美元上方窄幅震荡，价格波动极低，市场处于整理蓄势阶段，整体偏向横盘略多结构，短线观望为主等待方向选择信号  

MACD维持0.95正值，动能略偏多但未放量，SMA20在80.07形成短期支撑，RSI缺失使超买超卖难判，结构中性偏强  

价格区间收敛于80.30-80.77之间，上方阻力80.80及81.20，下方支撑80.30与80.00，突破需放量确认信号待观察  

短线建议维持高抛低吸策略，80.30附近分批吸纳，80.80上方减仓，若放量突破81则顺势跟进，控制风险。区

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
SOL/USD is trading around 80.54 after a tightly compressed session, with price fluctuating within a narrow 80.32–80.77 range. This behavior signals short-term equilibrium between buyers and sellers, with neither side able to establish clear dominance. The market remains in a consolidation phase following recent directional exhaustion. Price holding slightly above the 20-day SMA suggests early stabilization, keeping the broader short-term outlook neutral to mildly constructive.

The 20-day SMA at 80.07 is currently acting as a key reference point, with price oscillating just above it, reinforcing its role as dynamic support. MACD at 0.95 indicates positive momentum, though lack of signal data limits confirmation of trend strength. Still, the positive reading suggests underlying bullish pressure. Momentum remains fragile, and any shift below the SMA could quickly neutralize current bullish undertones.

Immediate support is located at 80.32, followed closely by the SMA near 80.07, forming a strong support cluster. A sustained break below this zone could expose downside risk toward 79.50. On the upside, resistance is established at 80.77, the session high. A breakout above this level could open room toward 81.20 and potentially 82.00, where prior supply pressure may re-emerge.

Trading conditions favor range strategies in the current environment, with mean reversion plays near support and resistance boundaries. Buying near 80.10–80.30 with tight stops below 80.00 may be viable while consolidation persists. Alternatively, breakout traders should wait for a decisive close above 80.80 before targeting higher levels. Risk management is essential given the compressed volatility and potential for sudden expansion.
